feed.title: Your awesome title
feed.subtitle: Write an awesome description for your new site here. You can edit this line in _config.yml. It will appear in your document head meta (for Google search results) and in your feed.xml site description.

entry.title: Welcome to Jekyll!
entry.content:
You’ll find this post in your _posts directory. Go ahead and edit it and re-build the site to see your changes. You can rebuild the site in many different ways, but the most common way is to run jekyll serve, which launches a web server and auto-regenerates your site when a file is updated.

Jekyll requires blog post files to be named according to the following format:

YEAR-MONTH-DAY-title.MARKUP

Where YEAR is a four-digit number, MONTH and DAY are both two-digit numbers, and MARKUP is the file extension representing the format used in the file. After that, include the necessary front matter. Take a look at the source for this post to get an idea about how it works.

Jekyll also offers powerful support for code snippets:

Check out the Jekyll docs for more info on how to get the most out of Jekyll. File all bugs/feature requests at Jekyll’s GitHub repo. If you have questions, you can ask them on Jekyll Talk.
entry.summary: You’ll find this post in your _posts directory. Go ahead and edit it and re-build the site to see your changes. You can rebuild the site in many different ways, but the most common way is to run jekyll serve, which launches a web server and auto-regenerates your site when a file is updated.

entry.title: Polymorphism
entry.content:
In this blog, I will talk about my understanding of polymorphism in C++. Polymorphism allows objects belonging to different types to respond to a method call of the same name, according to type-specific behavior. The type-specific behavior is determined at run-time (called later binding or dynamic binding).

There are typically two cases where polymorphism is used in C++. One is to use virtual functions. The other is to use abstract classes.

In the first case, how to use virtual function in C++?

In the first case, how to use virtual function in C++?

Suppose there is a base class and a derived class from the base class. Inside the derived class, there is a function which is modified and derived from the base class. We would create a vector of base class objects and store both base class objects and derived class objects in the vector. When loop over the vector and call the function, we would like to make compiler look at the type of objects to call the function, not look at the type of the vector to determine what function to call.

Before using polymorphism; for example, we have an employee class as a base class and a manager class as a derived class. There is a same name function (called grossPay) with different code defined in the employee class and the manager class. We create a vector of base class. We store both employee objects and manager objects in the vector. We loop over the vector items and use the item to call their member function 'grossPay'. We want the compiler figure out at runtime the correct type of item objects it is calling the function for. This does not work before using polymorphism; the compiler looks at the type of the vector(employee) to determine what function to call, rather than the type of the objects(manager) to determine what function to call.

How to implement polymorphism in Object-Oriented Programming?

How to implement polymorphism in Object-Oriented Programming?

To use polymorphism, we must make the function in question(grossPay) defined in the base class(employee class) a virtual function by typing the keyword 'virtual' before the return data type. It is not required but it is recommended that we make the function in question (grossPay) defined in the derived class(manager class) a virtual function. This would be easier for the code reader to read the code.

Now, we have made the grossPay function virtual in both base class(employee class) and derived class. Next, we have to use pointers and need the vector is a vector of the base class pointers(employee pointers).

Then, when calling the function, the compiler will look at the function(grossPay), know the function is virtual and determine which code to run based on the type of objects it comes across and stored in the vector(or variable), rather than the data type the vector(or variable) was declared as.

The advantages of the polymorphism is that by making the function in question in the base class polymorphic, we can then store objects of any types a base class and the derived class in a single container, and call the correct function for that object based on the type of object that it is. It makes the program much more flexible especially when we are dealing with objects of related classes.

The corresponding source codes are named "binaryfilewrite.cpp", "binaryfileread.cpp" and "binaryfileRW.cpp".

The programming test results are added in the end of the corresponding cpp files.

The source codes are available in Github, the repository "File-Input-Output" .

The other way to use polymorphism in C++

The other way to use polymorphism in C++ is the idea of the abstract class. Many times when we working with polymorphism and inheritance, we want to create a class that cannot be instantiated, instead used as a base class and a model for all the derived classes.

A class that cannot be instantiated or will not be instantiated is called an abstract class. The abstract class is a class that has function declarations but not executable code in them.

For example, I can have a Shape class that is not a particular shape. Inside the class, there are a set of methods which are virtual function and can only be implemented with specific types of Shapes such as a draw function for a Circle or a draw function for a Square, rather than a draw function for a Shape.

How to create abstract classes in C++?

How to create abstract classes in C++?

The technique is to make the base class abstract. This is done by creating a set of methods and make them all pure virtual functions by assigning an initializer of zero to the functions. In this way, the base class then becomes an abstract class. The compiler will report error when we try to instantiate the base class because the base class is an abstract class.

The way to use the abstract class is to create derived classes which are derived from the base class. In the derived classes, we have to implement all of the pure virtual functions defined in the base class. Each derived functions in the derived classes for these pure virtual functions will implement its own version of the function which is where polymorphism comes in. The compiler will understand the right version of the derived function at runtime according to different types of derived classes.

The corresponding source codes are named "binaryfilewrite.cpp", "binaryfileread.cpp" and "binaryfileRW.cpp".

The programming test results are added in the end of the corresponding cpp files.

The source codes are available in Github, the repository "File-Input-Output" .
entry.summary: In this blog, I will talk about my understanding of polymorphism in C++. Polymorphism allows objects belonging to different types to respond to a method call of the same name, according to type-specific behavior. The type-specific behavior is determined at run-time (called later binding or dynamic binding).

entry.title: ExceptionHandling
entry.content:
In this blog, I will talk about the concept of Exception Handling.

Concepts

Errors in a program is also called exceptions. Exception Handling is very necessary if you want to avoid the error to cause a program crash. For example, if divide a number by a zero value, it will lead the program stop working and crash. We can handle exceptions usually by try and catch statements

There are two ways of handling exceptions. One way is to define an error(exception) to be a global constant value. The other way is to define an exception class which can be a derived class based on one of the standard C++ exceptions. In the second way, we need to bring in a header file, using '#include', '' stands for the standard exception header in C++. </p>

How to deal with the exception handling in C++?

How to use the try and catch block to deal with the exception handling in C++?

The first way

In the first way; 1st, create a global constant value to define an error, the value can be any number. 2nd, to keep the error from occurring, we put the code that we believe can lead to an error inside the try block. Inside try, if the error happens, we throw the error(exception), else, we do the normal code. 3rd, below the try block, we use catch block. Before entering the inside of the catch block, catch can take an argument which is passed in the exception thrown. Inside the catch block, if the argument equals to the defined global error(exception), then we can report the error warning for user by displaying the error information in the screen. Normally, it is good to write the errors using 'cerr', not write the errors to the standard output using 'cout'.

In this way, we test the error, throw the error exception, catch the exception and report the error information to user. This is the overview of how exception handling works. It makes us not crash the program when running a program.

The corresponding source codes are named "binaryfilewrite.cpp", "binaryfileread.cpp" and "binaryfileRW.cpp".

The programming test results are added in the end of the corresponding cpp files.

The source codes are available in Github, the repository "File-Input-Output" .

The second way

The other proper way to throw exceptions is by creating an exception class which can be derived from one of the standard C++ exceptions, say, runtime_error. In this case, we have to use processor code; "#include".</p>

In the second way; 1st, bring in a header file in the program by adding '#include'. 2nd, create an exception class to define an error. The exception class is a derived class that inherits on one of the standard C++ exceptions; say, runtime_error, we can also look up the C++ reference what the other standard exceptions are. Inside the class, the constructor is defined as a call the constructor for the base class(one of the standard C++ exceptions), in this call of the base class constructor, a customized message is passed in, which we want to be displayed for user when the error happens. When the exception object is called, it is thrown, the message will be displayed and the user will know what is wrong. 3rd, to keep the error from occurring, we put the code that we believe can lead to an error inside a try block. Here, we can enclose the code in a function. Inside the function, if the error happens, we throw the exception object, else, we return the calculated value in the function. Then, we put a possible exception causing statement, which is the call of the function, in the try block. 4th, below the try block, we use catch block to catch the exception object that has been thrown. Before entering the inside of the catch block, catch can take an argument which can be passed in the thrown exception object, so the catch argument is declared as the exception object reference. Inside the catch block, the exception object is stored in the object reference variable, and then call the member function 'what()' derived from the base standard exception class. The function 'what()' returns the argument passed in the exception class constructor function definition. The exception constructor is defined through calls of the standard exception base class and passing of the customized message argument. Using the calls of the 'what()'' function, we display the customized message to users in the catch code block. </p>

We want the error exception to occur when the error happens so that the program does not crash. You never ever want a program to crash, you always want to try to handle error exceptions in a program. Sometimes, we would like to give the user the opportunity to correct their input errors, we can put these codes handling error exceptions in a loop and set a termination value.

The other ways

There are situations where we might have more than one exception to catch.

In these situations, you can have multiple catches that you can make.

There can be situations, especially for large programs, where you cannot possibly know all the exceptions that can occur and you need to catch exceptions without knowing what exactly they are. The way to do it is to use 'catch(...){}' where the ellipsis is the argument to the catch statement. Inside the catch statement, we can warn user that an exception was thrown and it was caught in our catch all catch statement. If you know exactly what exceptions will be thrown, you do not need to use the catch all catch statement. In a large program, normally there will be a series of different specific catches for all of the exceptions you know can be thrown, and at the end of the series of catch statements, there can be one more catch all catch statement, where it is to catch any exceptions that were left out of the specific catch list. </p>

The corresponding source codes are named "binaryfilewrite.cpp", "binaryfileread.cpp" and "binaryfileRW.cpp".

The programming test results are added in the end of the corresponding cpp files.

The source codes are available in Github, the repository "File-Input-Output" .
entry.summary: In this blog, I will talk about the concept of Exception Handling.

entry.title: Class Templates
entry.content:
Generic Programming is that there is no specified data types when using algorithms. It means algorithm can apply to many types of data structures.

Concepts
entry.summary: Generic Programming is that there is no specified data types when using algorithms. It means algorithm can apply to many types of data structures.

entry.title: Function Templates
entry.content:
Generic Programming is that there is no specified data types when using algorithms. It means algorithm can apply to many types of data structures.

Benefits: generic programming can reduce the repeating codes.

There are two types of templates, one is Function Template, the other is Class Template

Function Templates vs Overloaded Functions

Overloaded Functions

  • Overloaded functions are functions which have the same function name but have different parameters.

  • Different parameters means either overloaded functions have a different number of parameters or any of their parameters are of a different typle.

  • A function cannot be overloaded only by its return type, it should have different parameters with another overloaded function.

Function Templates

  • Two functions with the same name and having the same function body, C++ can define these functions with generic types and has a same algorithm, called function templates.

  • When defining a function template, a keyword 'template' is used before defining the function to indicate you are defining a function template.

  • A function template has a series of template parameters, which can be regular types or generic type parameters by specifying the keywords either the 'class' or 'typename' followed by an identifier.

  • Syntax

Examples of function templates

Question: how to use function templates?

Example codes:

Notes:

  • From the example codes, function template can be overloaded if their template parameters (type parameters) are different, which is because they have a different number of template parameters.

  • Recall the regular overloaded functions in C++, they are different functions with the same name but their function parameters are different; either because they have a different number of parameters, or because any of their parameters are of a different type.

Question: how does C++ knows which function or function template to call?

There is a calling order for C++ compiler when calling overloaded functions, function templates. The choosing order for compiler is as follows.

  • 1st, Find a function where its parameters are matched exactly; a function not by instantiation of the function template.

  • 2nd, Find a function template where its template parameters are matched.

  • 3rd, Find a function where the calling function's parameters type can automatically convert to its function parameters and be matched.

  • 4th, if no one suitable function or function template can be found to match the calling function's parameters, then, the C++ compiler report the function calling error.

An example code that shows the calling order of function templates, overloaded functions.

The source codes are available in Github, the repository “……..” .
entry.summary: Generic Programming is that there is no specified data types when using algorithms. It means algorithm can apply to many types of data structures.

entry.title: Input/Output with files
entry.content:
Data can be stored in the form of files. Writing all data records into a file sequentially can form a sequential file. Sequential files are essentially sequential streams.

Concepts

In C++ standard library, there are mainly three stream classes, that is, ifstream, ofstream, fstream. These stream classes can be used to do reading and writing operation on the files, they can be called file stream classes.

  • ifstream: read data from files; compared to reading/input data from standard input device(keyboard).
  • ostream: write data into files; compare to writing/output data from standard output device(computer
    • screen).
  • fstream: read and/or write data from files.

Their relations are shown in the screenshot below:

How is the input/output with files done?

1. Open files

  • Specify the file name and relate file stream object to the file by creating a ifstream / ofstream/ iofstream object.

  • Specify the way how you would like to use files; for example,

    ios::out || open for file output operation and replace the original data in the file

    ios::in || open for file input operation

    ios::binary || open the file in binary mode

    ios::app || open for file output operation and append the content to the current content of the file

    ios::ate || set the initial position at the end of the file. If it is not set, the initial position is at the beginning of the file.

    ios::trunc || if the opened file for output operations already existed, its previous content is deleted and replaced by the new one.

  • After opening a file by relating a stream object to a specified physical file, it is common to check conditions before using the file(read/write from/to a file).

2. Read/Write files

  • Use a file stream pointer to identify the the current location of the element to be read and to write in the files.

  • Operation on files will happen where the stream pointer point to.

3. Close files

  • After input and output operations on a file is finished, the file should be closed by calling the file stream object's member function 'close( )'.

  • After that, the file stream object can be reused by another file, and the file can be available to be opened by relating to another file stream object.

Example Codes

1. Two ways of opening/creating a file using file stream

2. Codes relating to set up writing file stream pointer position before writing on files

3. Codes relating to set up reading file stream pointer position before reading from files

4. Reading/Writing operations on Binary files

Reading and writing operations on binary files is performed by using the file stream member functions: write, read

  • Syntax

  • Writing operation on binary files

  • Reading operation on binary files

5. Reading/Writing operations on Text files

When opening a file using file stream, if the 'ios::binary' flag is not included in their opening mode, then the file stream we are using is text file stream.

The reading and writing operations on text files are performed in the same way that we operated with cin (read from standard input stream)

and cout (write to standard output stream).

Example projects on Github

Three examples of tasks regarding the input/output operations on binary files are illustrated.

  • Task1: input scores and names of students from keyboard, and store these data in a binary file with the name "students.dat".

  • Task2: read the students data from the file "students.dat", and output these data to the screen.

  • Task3: modify some students data from the file "students.dat", and change student "Jack" to "Mike", change "Jane 40" to "Grace 70",
    and output the students data to the standard output device.

The corresponding source codes are named "binaryfilewrite.cpp", "binaryfileread.cpp" and "binaryfileRW.cpp".

The programming test results are added in the end of the corresponding cpp files.


The source codes are available in Github, the repository "File-Input-Output" .
entry.summary: Data can be stored in the form of files. Writing all data records into a file sequentially can form a sequential file. Sequential files are essentially sequential streams.

entry.title: inheritance
entry.content:
In this blog, I will talk about the concept of inheritance.

Example1

Concepts

In C++ standard library, there are mainly three stream classes, that is, ifstream, ofstream, fstream. These stream classes can be used to do reading and writing operation on the files, they can be called file stream classes.

  • ifstream: read data from files; compared to reading/input data from standard input device(keyboard).
  • ostream: write data into files; compare to writing/output data from standard output device(computer
    • screen).
  • fstream: read and/or write data from files.

Their relations are shown in the screenshot below: [![Image from Gyazo](https://i.gyazo.com/cb218b166acecb6f3f21df43cd232d89.png)](https://gyazo.com/cb218b166acecb6f3f21df43cd232d89)

How is the input/output with files done?

1. Open files

  • Specify the file name and relate file stream object to the file by creating a ifstream / ofstream/ iofstream object.

  • Specify the way how you would like to use files; for example,

    ios::out || open for file output operation and replace the original data in the file

    ios::in || open for file input operation

    ios::binary || open the file in binary mode

    ios::app || open for file output operation and append the content to the current content of the file

    ios::ate || set the initial position at the end of the file. If it is not set, the initial position is at the beginning of the file.

    ios::trunc || if the opened file for output operations already existed, its previous content is deleted and replaced by the new one.

  • After opening a file by relating a stream object to a specified physical file, it is common to check conditions before using the file(read/write from/to a file).

2. Read/Write files

  • Use a file stream pointer to identify the the current location of the element to be read and to write in the files.

  • Operation on files will happen where the stream pointer point to.

3. Close files

  • After input and output operations on a file is finished, the file should be closed by calling the file stream object's member function 'close( )'.

  • After that, the file stream object can be reused by another file, and the file can be available to be opened by relating to another file stream object.

Example Codes

1. Two ways of opening/creating a file using file stream

2. Codes relating to set up writing file stream pointer position before writing on files

3. Codes relating to set up reading file stream pointer position before reading from files

4. Reading/Writing operations on Binary files

Reading and writing operations on binary files is performed by using the file stream member functions: write, read

  • Syntax

  • Writing operation on binary files

  • Reading operation on binary files

5. Reading/Writing operations on Text files

When opening a file using file stream, if the 'ios::binary' flag is not included in their opening mode, then the file stream we are using is text file stream.

The reading and writing operations on text files are performed in the same way that we operated with cin (read from standard input stream)

and cout (write to standard output stream).

Example projects on Github

Three examples of tasks regarding the input/output operations on binary files are illustrated.

  • Task1: input scores and names of students from keyboard, and store these data in a binary file with the name "students.dat".

  • Task2: read the students data from the file "students.dat", and output these data to the screen.

  • Task3: modify some students data from the file "students.dat", and change student "Jack" to "Mike", change "Jane 40" to "Grace 70",
    and output the students data to the standard output device.

The corresponding source codes are named "binaryfilewrite.cpp", "binaryfileread.cpp" and "binaryfileRW.cpp".

The programming test results are added in the end of the corresponding cpp files.


The source codes are available in Github, the repository "File-Input-Output" .
entry.summary: In this blog, I will talk about the concept of inheritance.

entry.title: Apples
entry.content:
An apple is a sweet, edible fruit produced by an apple tree.

Apple trees are cultivated worldwide, and are the most widely grown species in the genus Malus. The tree originated in Central Asia, where its wild ancestor, Malus sieversii, is still found today. Apples have been grown for thousands of years in Asia and Europe, and were brought to North America by European colonists.
entry.summary: An apple is a sweet, edible fruit produced by an apple tree.

entry.title: Bananas
entry.content:
A banana is an edible fruit – botanically a berry – produced by several kinds of large herbaceous flowering plants in the genus Musa.

In some countries, bananas used for cooking may be called “plantains”, distinguishing them from dessert bananas. The fruit is variable in size, color, and firmness, but is usually elongated and curved, with soft flesh rich in starch covered with a rind, which may be green, yellow, red, purple, or brown when ripe.
entry.summary: A banana is an edible fruit – botanically a berry – produced by several kinds of large herbaceous flowering plants in the genus Musa.

entry.title: Kiwifruit
entry.content:
Kiwifruit (often abbreviated as kiwi), or Chinese gooseberry is the edible berry of several species of woody vines in the genus Actinidia.

The most common cultivar group of kiwifruit is oval, about the size of a large hen’s egg (5–8 cm (2.0–3.1 in) in length and 4.5–5.5 cm (1.8–2.2 in) in diameter). It has a fibrous, dull greenish-brown skin and bright green or golden flesh with rows of tiny, black, edible seeds. The fruit has a soft texture, with a sweet and unique flavor.
entry.summary: Kiwifruit (often abbreviated as kiwi), or Chinese gooseberry is the edible berry of several species of woody vines in the genus Actinidia.
